data.title: Toyota Motor reports FY results
data.description:
• Toyota Motor press release (NYSE:TM): FY Net income ¥3100B
  • Revenue of ¥48036B (+6.5% Y/Y).
  • Operating income : ¥4.8T
  • FY 2026: Operating income : ¥3.8T
  • Consolidated vehicle sales for this fiscal year was 9 million 362 thousand units, which was 99.1% of consolidated vehicle sales for the previous fiscal year.
  • Toyota and Lexus vehicle sales was 10 million 274 thousand units, which was 99.7% of such sales for the previous fiscal year due to supply constraints caused by the certification issue and other factors.

data.title: Legalzoom targets double-digit subscription revenue growth exiting 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: LegalZoom.com, Inc. (LZ) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Jeffrey Stibel stated that LegalZoom delivered strong Q1 results with revenue of $183 million, marking a 5% year-over-year increase. Subscription revenue grew by 8%, reflecting strength in compliance-related and virtual mail subscriptions, and this marks the first sequential acceleration in subscription revenue in over a year.
  • Stibel emphasized the importance of transitioning towards higher-end solutions, noting that over 60% of revenue is subscription-based, with nearly 90% of recurring customers on annual plans, providing long-term visibility and predictability.
  • CFO Noel Watson highlighted that adjusted EBITDA for Q1 reached $37 million, representing a 33% year-over-year increase. Adjusted EBITDA margin was 20%, benefiting from strong revenue performance and cost efficiencies.
  • LegalZoom reiterated its full-year outlook for 2025, projecting 5% revenue growth and an adjusted EBITDA margin of 23%. Subscription revenue is expected to achieve double-digit growth by Q4.
  • Stibel announced the integration of Formation Nation, which contributed $8.6 million in Q1 revenue, and is expected to bolster LegalZoom's market share in the lower-end formation space through targeted marketing efforts and enhanced offerings.
  • The company launched new subscription features, including enhancements to compliance-related services, which aim to improve customer engagement and upsell opportunities.

Outlook

  • LegalZoom reaffirmed its guidance for approximately 5% revenue growth for 2025, with strong confidence in achieving its target of double-digit subscription revenue growth by Q4.
  • For Q2, revenue is expected to range between $181 million to $185 million, reflecting 3% growth at the midpoint. Adjusted EBITDA for Q2 is projected in the range of $37 million to $41 million, with a margin of 21% at the midpoint.
  • The company plans to continue leveraging its pricing strategies and product enhancements to maintain growth momentum, while navigating a challenging macroeconomic environment.
  • Management highlighted an ongoing focus on cost controls and operational efficiencies to support profitability despite macroeconomic pressures.

Financial Results

  • Transaction revenue increased 1% year-over-year to $67 million, supported by contributions from Formation Nation and a higher volume of annual report filings.
  • Subscription revenue grew 8% to $116 million, driven by strong execution in compliance-related subscriptions and partnerships such as the 1-800Accountant initiative.
  • Deferred revenue increased by $36 million compared to Q4 2024, reflecting benefits from subscription pricing initiatives and the incorporation of Formation Nation's deferred revenue.
  • Gross margin improved to 67%, up 300 basis points year-over-year, primarily due to lower filing fees and operational efficiencies.
  • Free cash flow was $41 million, a 67% increase compared to Q1 2024, supported by higher adjusted EBITDA and subscription growth.

Q&A

  • Ella Smith, JPMorgan Chase, asked about the drivers behind the strong subscription unit growth. CEO Stibel explained that the growth was driven by bundling subscription products into premium formation packages and shifting focus toward higher-value customers. CFO Watson added that this strategy is expected to moderate as renewal rates normalize.
  • Pat McIlwee, William Blair, inquired about the impact of marketing adjustments and Formation Nation integration on spend levels. Stibel clarified that overall marketing spend would remain flat, with reallocations focused on more efficient channels and brand messaging.
  • Andrew Boone, Citizens, questioned the sustainability of double-digit subscription revenue growth. Stibel expressed confidence, highlighting strategic product enhancements and pricing adjustments as key drivers.
  • Elizabeth Porter, Morgan Stanley, asked about the company’s approach to pricing changes. Stibel stated that LegalZoom is carefully aligning pricing with added value to ensure customer retention and long-term growth.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed cautious optimism, focusing on subscription growth and the integration of Formation Nation. Concerns were raised about market share shifts and macroeconomic impacts.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing adaptability, operational discipline, and strategic progress toward long-term goals. Stibel noted, "We are concentrating on the areas of our business we can control."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone reflected increased assurance in achieving subscription growth targets.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Subscription revenue growth accelerated sequentially, marking a positive shift from the previous quarter.
  • Adjusted EBITDA margin increased by 400 basis points year-over-year, reflecting ongoing cost efficiencies.
  • Analysts' focus shifted from macroeconomic pressures to the sustainability of subscription growth and the strategic integration of Formation Nation.
  • Management's confidence in achieving long-term goals remained consistent, supported by strong Q1 performance metrics.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted potential headwinds from shifting macroeconomic conditions, including a decline in Census EIN applications and regulatory changes affecting business formations.
  • Analysts raised concerns about customer churn from lower-priced bundled subscriptions and the impact on ARPU.
  • Management underscored its focus on operational flexibility and cost controls to mitigate risks and drive profitability.

Final Takeaway

LegalZoom demonstrated strong Q1 performance, driven by subscription revenue growth and cost efficiencies. The company remains focused on achieving double-digit subscription revenue growth by Q4 2025 and integrating Formation Nation to expand its market presence. Management expressed confidence in navigating macroeconomic challenges while maintaining profitability and delivering shareholder value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Asia markets rise on global trade optimism; U.S. futures jump as Trump hints at major trade deal
data.description:
Asia stock markets rose on Thursday as investors reacted to President Donald Trump’s announcement on Truth Social that a news conference would be held Thursday to unveil a deal with a “big” country, reportedly the United Kingdom. Meanwhile, he stated that tariffs on China would remain in place ahead of US-China talks scheduled in Switzerland this weekend.

Meanwhile, Trump said he would not lower tariffs on China as a condition to begin trade negotiations ahead of US-China talks in Switzerland this weekend.

Caution also grew ahead of key Chinese economic data, including April trade figures and CPI/PPI reports. 

Gold rose to around $3,390 per ounce on Thursday, trimming losses from the previous session.

Japan (NKY:IND) rose 0.48% to above 36,900 on Thursday, reversing losses from the previous session, with technology stocks leading the gains. The Japanese yen strengthened to around 143.6 per dollar on Thursday, recouping some losses from the previous session.

On the domestic front, minutes from the latest Bank of Japan meeting indicated that the central bank would continue raising interest rates if its economic and inflation projections materialize. 

China (SHCOMP) rose 0.23% to around 3,350 while the Shenzhen Component climbed 0.6% to 10,160 on Thursday, extending recent gains, buoyed by fresh stimulus measures and optimism surrounding upcoming US-China trade talks, the offshore yuan fell to around 7.23 per dollar on Thursday, marking its third consecutive session of losses, as uncertainty persisted over the trajectory of US-China trade negotiations.

The People’s Bank of China on Thursday injected CNY 158.6 billion into the banking system through seven-day reverse repos at a reduced rate of 1.4%, down from 1.5% the previous day.

Hong Kong (HSI) rose 0.56% to 22,881 in early Thursday trading, extending gains for the sixth straight session.

The Hong Kong Monetary Authority kept its base rate unchanged at 4.75% on May 8, following the U.S. Federal Reserve's decision to maintain its key benchmark at the 4.25%–4.50% range for the third consecutive meeting. 

India (SENSEX) rose 0.07% trading around 80,709 in early deals on Thursday, after marginal gains in the previous session. Gains in technology, consumer durables, and financial services were offset by declines in the healthcare and communication sectors. Investor sentiment remained cautious amid escalating tensions between India and Pakistan.

Australia (AS51) rose 0.18% toward 8,200 on Thursday, extending gains from the previous session. The Australian dollar climbed above $0.645 on Thursday, recovering roughly half of the previous session’s losses as optimism surrounding global trade developments lifted sentiment. 

In the U.S., on Wednesday, all three major indexes ended higher as investors weighed the Federal Reserve’s decision to hold interest rates steady and monitored developments in US-China trade tensions.

U.S. stock futures jumped on Thursday as Trump hints at major trade deal: Dow +0.57%; S&P 500 +0.76%; Nasdaq +1.07%.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

More on Asia:

Trump set to unveil trade deal with UK on Thursday - report

China service growth hits 7-month low as trade disruptions weighs on new business orders

Australia private sector expands at 51 in April, services sector expands for 15th straight month

Australia inflation gauge eases slightly in April by 0.6%

Australia Q1 producer price inflation beats estimates; retail sales rises 0.3% in March

data.title: Germany records trade surplus of €21.1B in March
data.description:
• Germany recorded a trade surplus of €21.10B in March 2025. 
  • In March 2025, German exports increased by 1.1%, calendar- and seasonally adjusted, and imports decreased by 1.4% compared to February 2025.
  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR) (DAX:IND)    
  • Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Deutsche Bank expands defense sector focus with dedicated deal team
data.description:
Deutsche Bank (NYSE:DB) is significantly increasing its involvement in the defense industry, including establishing a dedicated team to focus on defense-related deals, Fabrizio Campelli, head of corporate and investment banking at Germany’s largest lender told Bloomberg.

Frankfurt-based Deutsche Bank (NYSE:DB) formed a bank-wide, cross-product defense and infrastructure working group in March, Campelli said. 

Since its launch, the group has initiated around 20 new deals across equity capital markets, mergers and acquisitions, and financing. A spokesperson for the bank said the core team includes approximately 30 bankers, as per the report.

data.title: Trump set to unveil trade deal with UK on Thursday - report
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump is expected to announce a trade agreement with the United Kingdom on Thursday, May 8, according to The New York Times report, citing sources familiar with the matter.

Trump had posted on Truth Social that he would hold an Oval Office news conference on Thursday about a "major trade deal" with a "big, and highly respected country."

Officials indicated that they are not considering removing the blanket 10% tariff as part of a trade deal, according to the report. Besides, Britain is subject to a 25% tariff on steel, aluminum, and automobiles, levies that British officials have been pushing their U.S. counterparts to lift.

Several reports indicate that the announcement will likely outline a memorandum of understanding or a framework for further negotiations rather than a fully detailed trade agreement.

The U.S.-UK deal could potentially involve reduced tariff quotas on British steel and automobiles and may also address tariffs on agricultural products. Britain was not among the countries hit with additional tariffs, as it imports more from the U.S. than it exports. 

On Tuesday, Trump said that he and top administration officials would review potential trade deals over the next two weeks to decide which ones to accept.  The administration appears to be nearing deals with India and Israel and is continuing to negotiate with South Korea, Japan, Vietnam and other nations. Separately, senior officials from the U.S. and China are set to meet in Geneva, Switzerland. 

Earlier this week, Britain signed a free trade agreement with India—the largest such pact since exiting the European Union. 

The pound (GBP:USD) and FTSE 100 (UKX) stock-index futures have rallied amid hopes the UK will secure a trade deal with the U.S. 

ETFs to track broader U.S. market: (SP500): (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

ETFs to track UK market : (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Novagold prices $179 million upsized public offering
data.description:
• Novagold Resources (NYSE:NG) (TSX:NG:CA) Thursday announced pricing of its public upsized offering of 47,850,000 common shares at a price to the public of $3.75 per share.
  • In addition, Novagold Resources (NYSE:NG) has granted the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to 7,177,500 additional common shares at the public offering price, less underwriting discounts and commissions.
  • Gross proceeds from the upsized offering are expected to be approximately $179.4 million.
  • The stock price slumped 5% on Tuesday during after-market hours of trading.

data.title: Kratos highlights 10% revenue growth forecast for 2025 driven by hypersonics and tactical drones
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions (KTOS) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Eric DeMarco highlighted a strong performance in Q1 with a book-to-bill ratio of 1.2:1 and reaffirmed confidence in achieving 10% organic revenue growth for 2025 and 13%-15% for 2026. The company’s opportunity pipeline reached an all-time high of $12.6 billion.
  • The hypersonic franchise remains a primary growth driver, supported by advancements in systems like Erinyes and the newly successful Dark Fury hypersonic vehicle. Investments in additional production capacity are ongoing to meet projected demand.
  • Tactical drones, including Valkyrie, continue to progress, with new customer contracts and operational expansions cited. Internal investments in serial production of Valkyrie drones have been pivotal in meeting customer requirements.
  • CFO Deanna Lund reported Q1 revenues of $302.6 million, surpassing prior guidance, and adjusted EBITDA of $26.7 million, driven by high-margin revenues in key business segments.

Outlook

  • Management expects Q2 performance to reflect downtime related to the relocation of the Israeli microwave facility, but confidence remains strong in achieving full-year guidance.
  • The company forecasted 10% organic revenue growth for 2025 and 13%-15% growth for 2026. Hypersonic systems and tactical drones are expected to lead this growth, with hypersonics anticipated to drive significant revenue in 2026.
  • Lund noted ongoing cost pressures in multi-year fixed-price contracts but emphasized efforts to renegotiate future contracts at higher rates to offset inflation impacts.

Financial Results

  • Organic revenue growth across business units ranged from 13% to 18%, with strength in Microwave Products, C5ISR, and Defense Rocket Support businesses.
  • Free cash flow used in operations was $51.8 million, reflecting investments in inventory and facility expansions. Capital expenditures for the quarter were $22.6 million.
  • The Unmanned Systems segment reported 6.2% organic revenue growth, while KGS achieved 7.8% growth, excluding the impact of the recent Norden Millimeter asset acquisition.

Q&A

  • Peter Arment, Baird: Questioned about the Golden Dome initiative and its integration with Kratos' space and satellite systems. DeMarco emphasized Kratos' leadership in ground systems and space domain awareness, citing strong funding opportunities in this segment.
  • Mike Crawford, B. Riley Securities: Asked about turbojet engine production and associated missile programs. DeMarco identified several missile programs utilizing Kratos engines, underscoring the growth potential in this area.
  • Ken Herbert, RBC Capital Markets: Inquired about the profitability of Unmanned Systems in 2025. Lund confirmed expectations for continued profitability, emphasizing cost management efforts.
  • Andre Madrid, BTIG: Requested updates on tactical drone bookings. DeMarco stated that target drones dominate current bookings, but tactical drones are expected to gain momentum with additional contracts anticipated later in the year.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised concerns about cost pressures and the timing of major program milestones, reflecting a slightly cautious tone.
  • Management maintained a confident outlook, particularly emphasizing growth in hypersonics, tactical drones, and microwave electronics. DeMarco's statements, such as "we are confident" and "we expect," demonstrated a positive sentiment.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts appeared more focused on execution risks, while management's tone remained consistent, emphasizing strategic opportunities and growth drivers.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2025 remains unchanged at 10% organic revenue growth, with additional confidence in 2026 projections. The previous quarter also highlighted hypersonics and tactical drones as key drivers, but Q1 2025 included updates on successful Dark Fury missions and new tactical drone contracts.
  • Analysts' focus shifted slightly toward cost management challenges and the impact of facility relocations, while management highlighted advancements in hypersonic systems and expanded international opportunities.

Risks and Concerns

  • Cost increases in multi-year fixed-price contracts, particularly in Unmanned Systems, were noted as a challenge. Management is pursuing renegotiations and alternative suppliers to mitigate these impacts.
  • Relocation of the Israeli microwave facility poses a risk of temporary disruptions, though management expressed confidence in minimizing operational impact.
  • Dependence on timely government contract awards remains a concern, particularly under continuing resolution conditions.

Final Takeaway

Kratos Defense & Security Solutions reported strong Q1 results, driven by growth in hypersonics, tactical drones, and microwave electronics. The company reaffirmed its 2025 revenue growth projection of 10%, with hypersonics expected to lead further growth into 2026. Management emphasized strategic investments, operational expansions, and a robust opportunity pipeline as key enablers for sustained performance, while also addressing cost challenges in fixed-price contracts.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Anheuser-Busch InBev reports mixed Q1 results; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Anheuser-Busch InBev press release (BUD): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.81 beats by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $13.63B (-6.3% Y/Y) misses by $180M.
  • Volumes declined by 2.2%, with beer volumes down by 2.5% and non-beer volumes down by 0.2%.
  • 53% increase in Gross Merchandise Value from sales of third-party products through BEES Marketplace to reach 645 million USD.
  • In 1Q25, Normalized EBITDA increased by 7.9% to 4 855 million USD with a margin expansion of 218bps to 35.6%.

  • 2025 Outlook

    (i) Overall Performance: We expect our EBITDA to grow in line with our medium-term outlook of between 4-8%. The outlook for FY25 reflects our current assessment of inflation and other macroeconomic conditions.

    (ii) Net Finance Costs: Net pension interest expenses and accretion expenses are expected to be in the range of 190 to 220 million USD per quarter, depending on currency and interest rate fluctuations. We expect the average gross debt coupon in FY25 to be approximately 4%.

    (iii) Effective Tax Rate: We expect the normalized ETR in FY25 to be in the range of 26% to 28%. The ETR outlook does not consider the impact of potential future changes in legislation.

    (iv) Net Capital Expenditure: We expect net capital expenditure of between 3.5 and 4.0 billion USD in FY25.
